title: Buddhism and Parasitic Processing
essence: Having established a cognitive-scientific account of higher states of consciousness, Vervaeke turns to the tradition most systematically focused on them: Buddhism. He begins by flagging the interpretive crisis, Batchelor's argument that the core Buddha did not teach doctrines about rebirth and nirvana but rather issued four imperative provocations: comprehend suffering, let go of craving, experience cessation, cultivate the path. This reframing is crucial because it turns Buddhism from a metaphysical system into a set of actionable psycho-technologies. The key conceptual advance is the reinterpretation of dukkha: not 'all is pain' (the standard mistranslation) but the condition of a wheel off its axle, losing the grip of agency as one's cognitive machinery runs against itself. Vervaeke then introduces parasitic processing as his cognitive-scientific gloss on dukkha: the way normal adaptive heuristics (confirmation bias, availability, representativeness) feed on each other through affect to produce locked-in self-deceptive spirals from which no single intervention can escape. Marc Lewis's reciprocal narrowing completes the picture: as cognitive flexibility decreases, the available arena shrinks, which further decreases flexibility, in a tightening spiral that is the cognitive science of addiction, and by extension, of all forms of existential entrapment. The eightfold path is then reframed as a counteractive dynamical system operating simultaneously on cognition, character, and consciousness to break this spiral through reciprocal opening.
keyClaims: Batchelor's re-reading transforms Buddhism from a metaphysical doctrine into four active provocations: comprehend dukkha, let go of craving, realize cessation, cultivate the path.
Dukkha is not 'all is suffering' but the loss of agency, a wheel off its axle destroying itself, the condition of a self whose adaptive machinery has turned against itself.
Parasitic processing names the cognitive-science mechanism behind dukkha: normal adaptive heuristics feeding on each other through affect to produce inescapable self-deceptive spirals.

Marc Lewis's reciprocal narrowing describes how agent and arena co-contract in a tightening spiral until no option remains, the cognitive-science model of addiction extended to all existential entrapment.

The eightfold path is a counteractive dynamical system that simultaneously addresses cognition (right understanding, thinking), character (right speech, action, livelihood), and consciousness (right effort, mindfulness, concentration) to enable reciprocal opening rather than narrowing.

concepts.summary: Reinterpreted etymologically as a wheel off its axle, the loss of agency as adaptive machinery turns self-destructive, not a claim that all life is painful.
Vervaeke and Ferraro's concept: normal adaptive heuristics feeding on each other through affect to produce inescapable locked-in spirals of self-deception and self-destruction.
Lewis's model of addiction: decreasing cognitive flexibility shrinks the arena, which further decreases flexibility, in a tightening co-contraction between agent and arena.
Reframed as a counteractive dynamical system operating at three levels, cognition, character, consciousness, to produce reciprocal opening as the antidote to reciprocal narrowing.
Buddhism is analyzed as an ecology of psycho-technologies specifically designed to counter parasitic processing, Batchelor's reading makes this functional framing explicit.
Identified as the mechanism through which parasitic processing sustains itself: each adaptive heuristic reinforces the distorted framing that triggers the next heuristic.
Central to the reciprocal narrowing model: agent and arena co-contract, each tightening the other's constraints in the addiction or entrapment spiral.
Dukkha and parasitic processing are failures of relevance realization, the machinery that normally serves adaptation has been hijacked into self-reinforcing loops.
The attempt to meet being-needs (freedom from suffering, genuine agency) through having-mode strategies is the modal confusion driving parasitic processing.
Fromm's distinction applied: craving is having-mode applied to being-needs, which is what generates the self-destructive dynamic of parasitic processing.
Buddhism is positioned as an axial breakthrough: the first tradition to systematically theorize and counter the cognitive dynamics of existential self-destruction.

people.summary: The Buddha is the central figure: his four ennobling provocations are the framework, and Vervaeke's cognitive-scientific account is presented as a translation of what the Buddha was actually pointing at.

Batchelor's secular Buddhist re-reading is the hermeneutic lens through which Vervaeke approaches the texts, turning the Noble Truths into actionable imperatives and stripping away the metaphysical overlay.
Lewis's reciprocal narrowing model of addiction provides the cognitive-science formalization of parasitic processing, and his account of recovery as reciprocal opening mirrors the eightfold path.
Co-author of Vervaeke's published account of parasitic processing, referenced when naming and defining the concept.
Fromm's having-mode/being-mode distinction is applied to explain how craving drives dukkha by trying to satisfy being-needs through having-mode strategies.

crossRefs.context: The insight-of-consciousness account of HSC from ep-12 now gets its Buddhist application: the eightfold path is designed to produce exactly that kind of meta-cognitive breakthrough in the context of existential entrapment.
Parasitic processing and domicide together explain the Hellenistic crisis, the Epicureans, Cynics, and Stoics are responding to the same cognitive dynamics that the Buddhist analysis names.
Existential entrapment and the need for gnosis will be developed directly from the conditions described here as parasitic processing and reciprocal narrowing.

quotableMoments.quote: We called this in, when we published, 'Parasitic Processing'. It's not just about bad events. This is just one example. We get into all kinds of these spirals.
quotableMoments.context: Vervaeke naming the published concept of parasitic processing while emphasizing its generality, it is not just addiction but the cognitive science of all existential self-destruction.

quotableMoments.quote: The Eightfold Path is a counteractive dynamical system that counteracts parasitic processing and does reciprocal opening, beyond the ego self and beyond the everyday world where it's represented by an eight spoked wheel.
quotableMoments.context: The reframing of the eightfold path as a coordinated cognitive intervention system, not a list of moral rules but a dynamical counter-spiral.
